alerts.title: Fire Activity
alerts.description: Between US 24 and County Road 5A from Mile Point 0 to Mile Point 3. Road closed expect delays due to fire activity. Comment: Turn lanes from US-24 to CO-300 are closed due to fire activity.

alerts.title: Maintenance Operations
alerts.description: Between Exit 123: Shoshone (3 miles east of the No Name area) and Exit 129: Bair Ranch (6 miles west of the Dotsero area) from Mile Point 122.8 to Mile Point 126. The left lane is closed due to road maintenance operations. Slower speeds are advised.

alerts.title: Mud Slide
alerts.description:
Between Exit 97: Silt (2 miles east of the Silt area) and Exit 105: New Castle (near New Castle) from Mile Point 101.5 to Mile Point 105. The right lane is closed due to a mud slide. See map for detour(s). Comment: Traffic detoured via US-6. Please plan for delays this morning!
Between Exit 105: New Castle (near New Castle) and Exit 97: Silt (2 miles east of the Silt area) from Mile Point 105 to Mile Point 101.5. Road closed expect delays due to a mud slide. See map for detour(s). Comment: Traffic detoured via US-6. Please plan for delays this morning!

alerts.title: Other Activity
alerts.description: At Williams Street (Minturn) at Mile Point 145.1. Traffic impacts. There is alternating traffic. Slower speeds are advised. Comment: Utility work will begin on US-24, mile point 145.1 on Monday, July 20th though Sunday, September 6th. Roadway will be one-lane alternating with flaggers during the day, and opened to two lanes at night. Expect delays and allow extra time for your commute.

alerts.title: Paving Operations-139938
alerts.description: Between Exit 133: Dotsero (near Gypsum) and US 6 (near Eagle) from Mile Point 139 to Mile Point 147. Right lane closed due to paving operations. Width limit in effect. Width limit 11'0". Starting July 23, 2026 at 7:00AM MDT until July 23, 2026 at about 7:00PM MDT. Full schedule below: • July 23, 7:00AM - July 23, 7:00PM Comment: Intermittent single lane closures, peed reduced to 55 mph and 10-20 minute delays. For more information email: resurfacingproject@gmail.com.

planned_events.title: I-70 Glenwood Canyon bridge joints-138571
planned_events.description: Between Exit 116: CO 82; Glenwood Springs/Aspen (near Glenwood Springs) and Exit 133: Dotsero (2 miles west of the Dotsero area) from Mile Point 118.1 to Mile Point 129.9. Bridge construction. Starting July 23, 2026 at 7:00AM MDT until July 23, 2026 at about 7:00PM MDT. Full schedule below: • July 23, 7:00AM - July 23, 7:00PM Comment: Westbound I-70 will be reduced to one lane from near the Bair Ranch Rest Area (Exit 129) at 128.5 and before the Hanging Lake Rest Area (Exit 125), MP 126.5, to just for work on three bridge joints. Eastbound I-70 will be reduced to one lane from near the Shoshone Rest Area (Exit 123), MP 123, to the Hanging Lake Rest Area (Exit 125) for an additional three bridge joints. Daytime work hours will be 7 a.m. to 7 p.m., Monday through Friday. The speed limit will be reduced to 35 mph through the work zone.
